issues.title: Integer overflow in BuildPolyInput leading to heap buffer overflow
issues.problem: Both Pad16Len() and BuildPolyInput() have unchecked arithmetic that can overflow for large inputs. Pad16Len() at line 168 can wrap when len is close to UINT32_MAX. BuildPolyInput() at line 176 calculates total = padAad + padPt + GCM_BLOCKSIZE without overflow checks. This causes malloc to allocate a smaller buffer than needed, and subsequent memcpy_s operations use wrapped destMax values, leading to heap buffer overflow.

issues.title: DupCtx outPtr aliasing causes heap buffer overflow
issues.problem: MODES_GCM_SIV_DupCtx uses BSL_SAL_Dump to copy the entire struct including the outPtr field. The outPtr points to caller-allocated buffer. If the duplicated context is used for another encryption/decryption operation, it will write ciphertext to the original caller's buffer when GET_TAG is called, causing heap corruption since the original buffer was sized for the original message only.

issues.title: Integer overflow in MODES_GCM_SIV_Update msgLen causes heap overflow
issues.problem: The calculation modeCtx->msgLen + inLen at line 462 can overflow if many Update calls are made with large data. When overflow occurs, realloc allocates a smaller buffer than needed, and the subsequent memcpy_s and msgLen increment operate on incorrect sizes, causing heap buffer overflow.

issues.title: Deferred output writes can overflow caller buffers
issues.problem: Update() buffers all input internally, remembers only the first out pointer, and forces *outLen = 0. When GET_TAG is later called, it writes the entire buffered message to the raw outPtr without checking how much space the caller originally advertised. This violates the AEAD API contract where Update should process data incrementally and respect output buffer sizes.

issues.title: Tag copied even when encryption fails in GcmSivEncryptFinish
issues.problem: If AesCtrGcmSiv() fails during encryption, the function still copies the tag to the output buffer before returning the error. Callers who check the tag buffer but not the return value might use an unencrypted payload with a valid-looking tag, leading to security issues.

issues.title: AES-GCM-SIV encryption fails for zero-length plaintext
issues.problem: GcmSivEncryptFinish unconditionally checks if outPtr is NULL and returns an error, even when msgLen is 0. For zero-length plaintexts (e.g., when using AES-GCM-SIV only to authenticate AAD), it is valid for outPtr to be NULL since there is no ciphertext to write.

issues.title: AES-GCM-SIV decryption fails for empty ciphertext
issues.problem: Similar to the encryption path, GcmSivDecryptFinish rejects NULL outPtr even when ciphertext length is zero. This prevents verification of empty payloads where only AAD needs authentication.

issues.title: CstTimeTagCmp returns HPKE-specific error code
issues.problem: CstTimeTagCmp returns CRYPT_HPKE_ERR_AEAD_TAG when tag verification fails. This mixes module boundaries by returning an HPKE-specific error from a generic AES mode component. Generic EAL error handling logic may not correctly interpret this error code.

issues.title: GCM-SIV decryption breaks existing AEAD API contract
issues.problem: The implementation treats the last 16 bytes passed to Update() as the authentication tag. This is inconsistent with the existing CRYPT_EAL_Cipher AEAD API used by GCM/CCM/ChaCha20-Poly1305, where Update() processes ciphertext and CipherCtrl(GET_TAG) handles the tag separately via CRYPT_CTRL_SET_TAG. This creates a hidden special case that breaks generic AEAD callers.

issues.title: Missing newline at end of file
issues.problem: The header file is missing a terminating newline character, which violates POSIX standards and may cause issues with some tools.
issues.code:
#endif /* CRYPT_MODES_GCM_SIV_H */
\ No newline at end of file

issues.title: Missing newline at end of file
issues.problem: The source file is missing a terminating newline character, which violates POSIX standards.
issues.code:
#endif /* HITLS_CRYPTO_GCM && HITLS_CRYPTO_AES && HITLS_CRYPTO_GHASH */
\ No newline at end of file

issues.title: Missing newline at end of file
issues.problem: The test file is missing a terminating newline character.
issues.code:
/* END_CASE */
\ No newline at end of file
